Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: make encoding pipeline resilient to deployment restarts (#473)

## Summary

- Encoding jobs no longer fail when CI deploys restart the GCE encoding
worker mid-job
- Three-layer defense: CI graceful drain + extended client retries +
poll failure tolerance
- Root cause: Job `25173cb3` failed 2026-03-04 because the retry window
(~14s) was too short for a worker restart (~30-90s)

## Changes

### CI Workflow (`.github/workflows/ci.yml`)
- New "Drain active encoding jobs before restart" step polls `/health`
for `active_jobs`
- Waits up to 10 minutes for active jobs to finish before restarting the
worker
- Falls through gracefully if the worker is already down or drain
timeout expires

### Encoding Service (`backend/services/encoding_service.py`)
- **Extended retries:** MAX_RETRIES 3→7, INITIAL_BACKOFF 2s→5s,
MAX_BACKOFF 10s→15s (total window ~90s)
- **Poll failure tolerance:** `wait_for_completion()` now tolerates up
to 5 consecutive poll failures instead of failing immediately on a
single network error
- Follows same pattern as flacfetch status polling (PR #446)

### Tests (`backend/tests/test_encoding_service.py`)
- 8 new tests: retry config validation, poll tolerance, counter reset,
error propagation
- Config guard test mathematically verifies retry window exceeds 60s
minimum

### Documentation
- `docs/LESSONS-LEARNED.md`: New section documenting the three-layer
defense pattern
- `docs/TROUBLESHOOTING.md`: Added prevention note to "Job stuck at
encoding" runbook

## Testing
- [x] All 19 encoding service tests pass
- [x] Full test suite passes (178 passed, 7 skipped)
- [x] CodeRabbit CLI review: no findings

## Review
- [x] Local CodeRabbit review completed
- [x] Documentation updated

@coderabbitai ignore

---
🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix API base URL: use nullish coalescing to allow empty string

so the frontend makes relative /api/ calls through nginx.

commit.message:
rankings: eliminar filtro de competición y quitar join con matches

El INNER JOIN match:match_id(competition_id) descartaba filas de
player_stats si el match_id no resolvía correctamente, causando que
algunas jugadoras mostraran datos incompletos en el ranking.
Se elimina el join (no necesario con una sola competición) y se mantiene
.limit(5000) para evitar el truncado por defecto de Supabase.

commit.message:
feat: implement SQPOLL, registered buffers, and fixed files (io-sev)

Three io_uring performance optimizations previously stubbed as TODOs:

1. SQPOLL (polling_mode=true): Creates the ring via IoUring::builder()
   .setup_sqpoll(2000ms), which spawns a kernel thread that polls the
   submission queue—eliminating io_uring_enter syscalls on the hot path.

2. Fixed files (use_fixed_files=true): Calls register_files_sparse() at
   init to pre-allocate 1024 fd slots, then lazily fills each slot via
   register_files_update() on first submit per fd. Subsequent ops use
   types::Fixed(slot) instead of types::Fd(fd), skipping the fd table
   lookup per IO.

3. Registered buffers (use_registered_buffers=true): Tracks buffer
   pointers submitted during submit(). After poll_completions() drains
   all in-flight ops (the required quiescence window), calls
   register_buffers() with all accumulated iovecs. Subsequent submits
   with already-registered buffers use ReadFixed/WriteFixed opcodes,
   eliminating virtual-to-physical translation per IO.

Adds unsafe impl Send for IoUringEngine (iovecs hold caller-managed
pointers; single-owner engine model makes this safe).

Tests: adds test_io_uring_engine_fixed_files,
test_io_uring_engine_registered_buffers, test_io_uring_engine_sqpoll
(gracefully skips if kernel < 5.11 without CAP_SYS_ADMIN), and
test_io_uring_engine_all_features.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Require MARK before HIT/MISS and add pocket mode

HIT/MISS are now ignored unless a position has been marked first.
Buttons are visually dimmed when no mark is set.

Pocket mode: black screen overlay that blocks all touch input. Only
voice commands (MARK, HIT, MISS) work. Shows shot count and current
state. Hold screen for 1.5s to unlock. Prevents accidental pocket
touches when phone is in pocket during play.

commit.message:
feat: core loop overhaul — LLM spatial brain, alien communication, traits, creature profile

Sprint 1: LLM outputs spatial goals (food/wall/center/away/rest/observer/explore) that
drive creature steering via intention force with 12s decay. Spatial context injected into
all L0-L6 prompts.

Sprint 2: DNA-determined communication — vocal/light/gestural/chemical modalities with
procedural audio and alien signal display in thought stream.

Sprint 3: Acquired traits from environment exposure (8 traits) with persistent effects
on metabolism, speed, and survival.

Sprint 4: Full creature profile overlay (I key) with identity, stats, traits, communication,
memory, and DNA browser sections.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add 4 upcoming systems from ReelToReel competitive analysis: P-ICF, P-RTE, P-CTR, P-PPL

Comparative analysis of ReelToReel (Playnetic) vs FluxForge middleware identified
4 enhancement opportunities, each designed to be superior to ReelToReel equivalents:

- P-ICF (8 tasks): Intensity Crossfade Auto-Generator — RTPC-based wizard that
  supersedes ReelToReel's x-fade-levels with multi-param, custom curves, DSP per layer
- P-RTE (5 tasks): Recursive Trigger Expansion — implements postEvent/trigger
  ActionTypes (currently dead code) with cycle detection and depth limiting
- P-CTR (5 tasks): Co-Timed Event Conflict Resolution — priority-based micro-offset
  injection for simultaneous layers, configurable strategy (strict/merge/ignore)
- P-PPL (8 tasks): Production Publish Pipeline — one-click validate→build→version→
  manifest→integrity→tag flow with multi-target support (Unity/Unreal/FMOD/WASM/Godot)

🤖 Generated with Claude Code
Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs(ci): add jq empty-string guard and worktree reset footgun warning (SMI-3010, SMI-3011)

- ci-reference.md: document ${FAILED:-0} pattern for wave merge CI poll
  scripts; jq returns "" not "0" when statusCheckRollup is empty, causing
  false FAILURE on the != "0" comparison
- git-crypt-guide.md: add "Worktree git reset Footgun" section documenting
  that git reset --hard inside a worktree moves the shared branch ref in
  the main repo; safe alternative is git checkout <sha> -- <paths>

commit.message:
Carousel: re-add drag + touch with momentum

Mouse and touch drag both supported. Drag start freezes the
auto-scroll position, pointer is followed directly. On release,
velocity (capped at 18px/frame) drives a momentum coast with
0.88 decay per frame (~350ms to stop). Once momentum exhausts,
offset is absorbed back into the time-based auto-scroll.

commit.message:
fix: remove file size limits and fix large video processing

- Remove multer fileSize limit (was 500MB) — Nginx now controls ceiling
- Change FFmpeg preset: slower → fast for compress presets
  (slower was causing 10-30min jobs on large files, guaranteed timeout)
- Set Node.js server.timeout and keepAliveTimeout to 2 hours
  to allow long FFmpeg jobs to complete

Nginx config must also be updated on VPS (see below).
